title: Minimum Number of Coins to Reach Target

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat:
The input consists of two space-separated integers n (n <= 10^5) and target (0 <= target <= 500).

The second line contains n space-separated integers a (1 <= a <= 10^5).

The third line contains n space-separated integers w (1 <= w <= 10^5) representing the cost of each coin.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the minimum number of coins required to reach the target.
statement.notes:
Time and space complexity O(n)

Note that the target is at most 500, so you can use int to represent it.

examples.explanation: We should take 5 and 5 to reach target 10
We should take 10, 100, 10, 100, 10, 100 to reach target 100

hints.text: Use greedy algorithm to always pick the largest coin that is no greater than the remaining target.

editorial.title: Minimum Number of Coins to Reach Target

functionSignature.cpp: int solve(int n, int target, int* a, int* w)
functionSignature.java: int solve(int n, int target, int[] a, int[] w)
functionSignature.python: def solve(self, n, target, a, w) -> int:
functionSignature.javascript: int solve(int n, int target, int a[], int w[])
